title: Molecular Detection of Phytoplasmas and Xylella spp. in Quarantine Plants
author: Takao Ito
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vvme646

steps.instruction: 1. Extraction 1.1. Crude extraction 1.1.1. Put leaf petioles (Ôľú50mg), a metal beads, and 1mL extraction buffer into a tube. 1.1.2. 2,500 rpm 60 sec. (the Multi-beads shocker) 1.1.3. 9,000 x g 10min 4C 1.1.4. Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Next steps, or keep it in a freezer.

substeps.instruction: 1. Extraction
Crude extraction 1.
Put leaf petioles (Ôľú50mg), a metal beads, and 1mL extraction buffer into a tube. 1.
2,500 rpm 60 sec. (the Multi-beads shocker) 1.
Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Next steps, or keep it in a freezer.

steps.instruction: 1.2. Isopropanol precipitation 1.2.1. Add an equal volume of cold isopropanol to the crude extract and mix. 1.2.2. 20,000 x g 5 min 4C 1.2.3. Discard supernatants and dry pellets. 1.2.4. Suspend the pellet in one-fifth volume of TE. Next steps, or keep it in a freezer.

substeps.instruction: Isopropanol precipitation 1.
Add an equal volume of cold isopropanol to the crude extract and mix. 1.
Discard supernatants and dry pellets. 1.
Suspend the pellet in one-fifth volume of TE. Next steps, or keep it in a freezer.

steps.instruction: 3. Data analysis 3.1. Export data 3.2. Consider positives of PP/XL at Ct<45 and IC at Ct<40. 3.3. Refer the PDF file (a protocol in Japanese and English) in the Absract for detail.

substeps.instruction: 3. Data analysis
Export data
Consider positives of PP/XL at Ct<45 and IC at Ct<40.
Refer the PDF file (a protocol in Japanese and English) in the Absract for detail.
